enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps his May 5, 2014 day living in Boulder: a 10-mile bike ride to do a house cleaning job, researching and planning to buy a Yamaha WR 125 or 450 dirt bike using a bank line of credit, frustration at slowing his website progress due to limited time while working, and the accidental loss of his hamster through a hole in his deck.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: wants to get a Yamaha WR dirt bike (~$1800) using a bank line of credit to have fun riding in the mountains

stances.topic: working while running ambitious web projects
stances.position: finds it frustrating and disruptive to momentum when a day job limits coding time to only a few hours
feels bad but is somewhat resigned, plans to seal the deck and get another hamster

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was living in Boulder Viewlog on May 5, 2013, or maybe May 14.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday, I rode 10 miles out of town to do a house and then rode back on some dirt for the first time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I spent all afternoon researching motorcycles and think I want to get a Yamaha WR 126, a little cross motorbike for about $1800.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a line of credit from the bank for $5000 at 19.5% interest to pay back a friend and buy a motorcycle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It would be fun to motorbike all over the mountainside.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I did a little bit of work, but it's a bummer because I'm used to being ambitious and working on big projects, but now I only have a couple of hours a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Working on the website feels like traveling through time at light speed because everything changes so fast.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to finish what I'm working on now, even though it feels boring and I wonder what I'm doing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm looking forward to organizing my YouTube page and creating a bunch of videos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working with a clock today for the first time and starting at nine, but I got to sleep super early yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was groggy this morning because I didn't sleep much last night.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I brought my hamster out on the porch yesterday, but it escaped through a hole in the deck and is now under the deck.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to seal the hole with cement and paint it to make it hermetically sealed, then buy another hamster.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The escaped hamster is probably starving and dehydrating under the deck, but hamsters are smart and know their escape routes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's a bummer because the hamster is a little smart guy stuck in the pitch black, but hamsters are hamsters.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I looked around the deck but didn't see the hole until it was too late, and the hamster didn't see it either until it was running.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to do two houses today and get breakfast now.
